T3.cte:
WITH prod AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Description", '') AS "Job Role Description",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Skills and Experience", '') AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       t."Job Role Modified Date" AS "Job Role Modified Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region),
     uat AS (SELECT t."Job Role ID" AS "Job Role ID",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Title", '') AS "Job Role Title",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Description", '') AS "Job Role Description",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Identifier", '') AS "Job Role Identifier",
       NULLIF(t."Job Role Skills and Experience", '') AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       t."Job Role Created Date" AS "Job Role Created Date",
       t."Job Role Modified Date" AS "Job Role Modified Date",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_NAME", '') AS "CLIENT_NAME",
       NULLIF(t."CLIENT_REGION", '') AS "CLIENT_REGION"
       FROM UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region)

in_prod_not_uat: The Residential Care Worker is responsible for the provision of a consistent, safe, secure and supportive living and learning environment for Children and Young People under the Guardianship and Custody of the Minister.

A minimum Certificate IV in Child, Youth & Family Intervention or Certificate IV in Community Services Work or must obtain qualification within 12 months of starting the role 
 Experience in working with families and/or children 
 Knowledge of Child Safe Environments, Mandated Reporting and understanding of basic child development principles, childcare principles and family dynamics 
 Highly effective negotiation and communication skills in managing challenging and confronting situations and behaviours
 Demonstrated ability to work autonomously with minimal supervision 
 Demonstrated ability to work under pressure and organise a workload accordingly
 Good ability in using Information Technology and Microsoft Office programs

FUNCTIONS OF THE ROLEThe Social Connections Program provides friendship and social connection to older people who may be experiencing loneliness or isolation. Volunteer Home Visitors are matched with clients and provide regular visits, offering companionship and meaningful social interaction. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Duties and TasksConduct regular visits with a matched client at their home or agreed location.Provide companionship through conversation and shared activities.Maintain consistent communication with the client regarding visits.Advise Volunteer Coordinators of any changes, concerns or issues.ResponsibilitiesComply with RangeCare policies and Code of Conduct.Maintain confidentiality at all times.Communicate respectfully and professionally.Follow health and safety practices for home visits.Respect role boundaries and refer additional support needs to RangeCare.

Compulsory RequirementsCurrent Disability Worker Screening clearance.Friendly, warm and compassionate approach.Ability to listen actively and build rapport.Desired SkillsUnderstanding of the needs of older adults.Ability to build positive relationships and connection.

The Clinical Practitioner provides professional and client focused therapeutic services to individuals couples children and families experiencing emotional psychological and relationship challenges. The role supports clients to set goals build coping and communication skills and promote positive behavioural change and improved mental wellbeing. The Clinical Practitioner delivers high quality services in line with professional standards legislative requirements and Centacare values while contributing to collaborative practice accurate documentation and ongoing professional development.

•    Accredited education and training in psychology, social work, counselling, mental health or a similar discipline leading to a professional qualification that is recognised in Australia•    Membership with a relevant professional body (such as AASW, APHRA, PACFA)•    Experience and skills in provision direct of clinical services. Demonstrable experience in working with children, adolescents, and adults to address issues such as emotional and psychological trauma, anxiety, depression, life-stage issues, behavioural problems etc. •    Demonstrable ability in implementing intervention strategies based on sound theoretical frameworks. •    Experience and demonstrable skills in running group programmes with early intervention and wellbeing promotion focus •    Demonstrable experience and ability in liaising and working with stakeholders such as referring agencies•    Skills and experience in psychometric assessment of children •    Experience in working with couples and families within a relationship framework •    Skills and experience in psychosocial assessment, case formulation, and intervention planning•    Willingness to work within the Catholic identity and ethos of the organisation

The General Manager oversees the Manufacturing Teams performance, driving business growth and delivering on financial targets for the Australian region. This position contributes to and executes strategic plans aligning with the owners vision, while cultivating an environment of creativity, innovation, and continuous improvement. They will also facilitate and oversee the digital transformation of the business, leveraging industry 4.0 and 5.0 advancements and ensuring that the business is at the forefront of the industrys technological trajectory.

Duties and responsibilities include:


	Direct the performance of the Australian manufacturing teams, ensuring effectiveness and productivity.
	Develop and implement robust growth strategies, focusing on enhancing productivity, capability and improving quality.
	Spearhead the digital transformation of the business in alignment with industry 4.0 and 5.0 frameworks.
	Establish, implement, and maintain company policies and procedures.
	Manage HR systems and processes, including people development, performance management and succession planning.
	Ensure regulatory compliance with OHS, Quality, and Environmental standards and manage regulatory body relationships.
	Provide leadership and strategic direction to drive business growth.
	Facilitate professional development opportunities and coaching for team members.
	Act as a primary point of escalation and company representative for major projects.
	Collaborate with the Consultancyand Operations teams to achieve business targets.
	Lead the Manufacturingteam in supporting project timelines and addressing operational issues.

At least 15 years of leadership experience in a manufacturing or related industry, preferably within the art or creative sector.
	Proven strategic planning, financial planning, and budgeting skills.
	Comprehensive understanding of modern manufacturing techniques and digital transformation, including industry 4.0 and 5.0.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple priorities while focusing on strategic objectives.
	Experience in HR systems and processes, quality control, and environmental compliance.
	High ethical standards, accountability, and commitment to transparency.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to foster relationships across all levels.
	Experience leading and managing a multi-disciplinary team to achieve business objectives.
	Outstanding commercial skills and business acumen.
	Passion for art and its societal role, and resilience to handle pressure in a growing business.
	A commitment to continuously improving processes and practices in alignment with industry trends and standards.

The Travel & Administration Officer at Mornington Shire Council plays a crucial role in managing travel arrangements and providing comprehensive administrative support to ensure the smooth operation of the Council's activities.Key Responsibilities:Coordinate and manage all travel bookings for staff, including flights, accommodation, and ground transportation.Develop and maintain relationships with travel service providers to negotiate competitive rates and ensure quality service delivery.Assist in the preparation of travel itineraries and provide pre-travel briefings to staff regarding policies and procedures.Manage the administration of travel-related documentation, ensuring compliance with Council policies and relevant regulations.Monitor travel budgets, track expenditures, and prepare financial reports related to travel activities.Provide general administrative support including scheduling meetings, managing correspondence, and maintaining records.Assist with event planning logistics such as venue selection, catering arrangements, and materials preparation for workshops or conferences.Liaise with various departments within the Council to ensure effective communication regarding travel needs and administrative tasks.This position requires a detail-oriented individual who can handle multiple tasks efficiently while maintaining a high level of professionalism. The successful candidate will contribute significantly to enhancing operational efficiency within the Mornington Shire Council through effective management of travel logistics and administrative support functions.

Required skillsExcellent organisational and time management skillsStrong communication and interpersonal abilitiesProficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)Attention to detail and accuracy in documentationAble to manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectivelyKnowledge of travel booking systems and proceduresAbility to work independently as well as part of a teamProblem-solving skills with a proactive approachRequired experiencesPrevious experience in an administrative or travel coordination roleExperience managing travel logistics for employees or teamsFamiliarity with budget management and financial reporting processesExperience in liaising with external vendors and service providersA track record of providing excellent customer service supportExperience working within a local government or public sector environment is desirableDiverse experience in event planning or coordination is an advantage

PurposeThis position is responsible for ensuring accounts receivable and credit control functions are performed efficiently and effectively in accordance with company policies, procedures and accounting standards.Responsibilities Cash Management  Perform accurate daily bank statement reconciliations.Advise relevant staff (e.g., salesperson) of payments received. Entering daily cashbook transactions to Equip for: Vehicle receipts Debtor receipts Sundry receipts  Liaise with relevant staff/debtors regarding to unknown money received.  Credit Control/Trade Debtor Management  Review & process all customer credit applications including variations to existing customer accounts. Maintain and manage outstanding debtor accounts and take the appropriate recovery action for overdue debtors.For accounts exceeding 90 days and older, work with the branches to recover the debt, and if required forward details to external debt collectors. Distribute Debtor Ageing report to Branch Managers monthly.Ensuring all Credit Control policies are adhered to, including restricting further credit to debtors with overdue invoices. Prepare monthly Credit Control report of debtor status, risks and mitigation plan. Monthly reconciliation of accounts receivable ledger balancing to sub-ledger.Process monthly interest charges to overdue debtors.Distribute monthly debtor statements, invoices & reminders.Prepare Debtor Refunds as required. Other Assists in annual audits, ad-hoc tasks and special projects as directed. Participate in year-end stock take. Perform, as directed, other duties that are within the limits of the incumbents’ skill, competence, and training.

Qualifications & Experience Understands accounting fundamentals such as debits, credits, accounts receivable, accounts payable, and budgets Ability to use standard desktop applications such as Microsoft Office, Microsoft Word, and Microsoft Outlook. Knowledge of accounting systems Be an effective communicator, highly organised with effective time management and have strong teamwork skills. Professional qualification in Accounting/Business discipline preferred.

The Psychologist provides professional psychological assessment and therapeutic services to individuals couples children and families. The role supports clients to build coping skills promote positive behavioural change and improve mental health and wellbeing while delivering high quality evidence based services aligned with professional standards legislative requirements and Centacare values.

●    Accredited education and training in psychology●    Membership with a relevant professional body (such as AHPRA, APS)●    Experience and skills in the provision of direct clinical services●    Skills and experience in psychosocial assessment, case formulation, and intervention planning●    Demonstrable ability in implementing intervention strategies based on sound theoretical frameworks●    Experience in working with individuals, couples and families within a systemic framework●    Experience and demonstrable skills in running group programmes with early intervention and wellbeing promotion focus ●    Demonstrable experience and ability in liaising and working with stakeholders such as referring agencies●    Willingness to work within the Catholic identity and ethos of the organisation

The Accommodation Commercial Officer plays an important role at the Great Ocean Road Authority, responsible for managing accommodation services and commercial operations to enhance visitor experiences and support the overall objectives of the organisation.This position includes a variety of responsibilities, which may involve:Oversight of accommodation facilities, ensuring they meet operational standards and provide an exceptional guest experience.Developing and implementing strategies to promote accommodation offerings and optimise occupancy rates.Managing relationships with external partners, stakeholders, and local businesses to foster collaboration and enhance commercial opportunities.Conducting market research to identify trends, assess competitor offerings, and inform pricing strategies.Monitoring financial performance related to accommodation services, including budgeting, forecasting, and reporting on key performance indicators.Coordinating marketing initiatives that effectively promote accommodation options through various channels.Ensuring compliance with relevant regulations and policies pertaining to health, safety, and environmental standards in all accommodation operations.The Accommodation Commercial Officer will also be tasked with collecting feedback from guests to continuously improve services offered while promoting sustainability practices within the organisation. The successful candidate will play a vital role in supporting the Great Ocean Road Authority's vision of being a leading destination for visitors seeking unique experiences along one of Australia’s most iconic coastal routes.

Required skills:Strong communication and interpersonal skillsProficiency in contract negotiation and managementExcellent organisational and time management abilitiesFinancial acumen, including budgeting and financial reportingAbility to work collaboratively with diverse stakeholdersProficient in Microsoft Office Suite and relevant property management softwareProblem-solving skills with a proactive approach to challengesRequired experiencesA minimum of 3 years’ experience in accommodation or commercial property managementExperience in developing and implementing operational strategiesDemonstrated experience in managing vendor relationships and service contractsExperience in conducting market research and analysis for commercial opportunitiesA track record of successfully managing projects from inception to completionPrevious roles within the tourism or hospitality sector would be advantageous.

As an Expressions of Interest position, your role will involve indicating your interest in a particular job or project. This may involve submitting a resume, cover letter, or other documents to demonstrate your qualifications and experience. Additionally, you may be required to complete an application form, attend an interview, or provide references to support your candidacy for the position.

You can have working hours that fit your schedule
	You can have access to formal in-person and online training
	You can develop genuine connections with the people you help


Successful applications will be subject to a police background check.

What we are looking for:We are seeking a skilled and motivated team member to join our Municipal Services Department within the Technical Services Directorate to help maintain and enhance the town’s infrastructure and community facilities. In this hands-on role, you will undertake welding, construction, installation and maintenance of essential fixtures and amenities, including street signage, lighting, playground equipment, fencing, paving, and public facilities such as toilets and seating. You will also operate and maintain work vehicles and equipment safely and efficiently, follow procedures, work independently and as part of a team, and accurately report maintenance needs or repairs. This position plays a key role in ensuring our town remains safe, functional, and welcoming, while upholding Council’s values and commitment to delivering high-quality community services.

What You Will BringYou will bring proven experience in the safe operation of machinery and equipment, with a strong understanding of civil works construction and maintenance practices. Skilled in performing pre-operational checks and minor maintenance, you work efficiently both independently and as part of a team, ensuring tasks are completed safely and to a high standard. With excellent communication skills and attention to detail, you maintain accurate records, logs, and work papers. Physically capable and safety-conscious, you confidently work outdoors in all conditions while adhering to WHS requirements. Bringing a professional attitude and respect for the public and colleagues, you hold a current LR Class driver licence, ready to contribute to the efficient delivery of essential community services across the NT.Must Have: Current LR Class driver licence to be able to drive in the NT, Current Police Check

The Rostering Officer at Lutheran Disability Services plays a crucial role in ensuring that the staffing needs of the organization are met effectively and efficiently. This position involves coordinating and managing staff schedules to provide optimal support for clients while maintaining compliance with relevant regulations and policies.

Key Responsibilities:


	Develop, implement, and maintain effective rostering systems that align with service delivery requirements.
	Coordinate staff rosters to ensure adequate coverage across various programs and services.
	Monitor attendance, leave requests, and shift changes to optimize workforce allocation.
	Communicate with staff regarding roster changes, availability, and any scheduling issues.
	Collaborate with management to address staffing shortages or surpluses and adjust rosters accordingly.
	Maintain accurate records of staff hours worked, leave balances, and other relevant data.


The successful candidate will be proactive in problem-solving, possess strong organizational skills, and demonstrate a commitment to providing high-quality service to both staff and clients within the disability sector.

Required skills


	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Proficient in rostering software and scheduling tools
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
	Attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy under pressure
	Problem-solving skills and the ability to think critically
	Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment
	Understanding of relevant legislation and compliance requirements in disability services


Required experiences


	A minimum of 2 years experience in a rostering or scheduling role, preferably within the healthcare or disability sector
	Experience with workforce management systems or similar scheduling software
	Demonstrated experience in managing staff rosters effectively while adhering to budget constraints
	Previous experience working with diverse teams and understanding their needs in service delivery settings
	Familiarity with employee relations, including handling conflicts or issues related to staffing schedules

The Project Lead at Great Ocean Road Authority plays a pivotal role in ensuring the successful delivery of projects that enhance the region's natural beauty and recreational opportunities. This position is responsible for overseeing all aspects of project management, from initiation through to completion, while ensuring alignment with the organisation's strategic objectives. Key responsibilities include:Leading project planning sessions to define scope, objectives, and deliverables.Coordinating with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to ensure timely execution of project components.Managing project budgets, timelines, and resources effectively to meet organisational goals.Developing comprehensive project documentation including plans, schedules, and reports.Conducting regular progress meetings to communicate updates and address any emerging issues.Ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and industry standards throughout the project lifecycle.Facilitating stakeholder engagement processes to gather input and feedback on projects.Identifying risks and implementing mitigation strategies to minimise impact on project outcomes.Promoting a culture of continuous improvement by sharing lessons learned post-project completion.The Project Lead will also act as a mentor for junior team members, fostering a collaborative environment that encourages professional growth. This role is essential in maintaining the integrity of projects while delivering exceptional experiences for visitors to the Great Ocean Road region.

Required skillsStrong leadership and team management abilitiesExcellent communication and interpersonal skillsProficiency in project management methodologies and toolsAnalytical thinking and problem-solving capabilitiesAbility to manage multiple projects and meet deadlinesStrong organisational skills with attention to detailKnowledge of budget management and financial acumenCompetence in stakeholder engagement and relationship buildingRequired experiencesA minimum of 5 years of experience in project management or a related fieldDemonstrated experience in leading cross-functional teamsProven track record of delivering projects on time and within budgetExperience working with government or public sector organisations is preferredEvident experience in risk assessment and mitigation strategiesBackground in managing community engagement processes or consultationsPrevious involvement in environmental or infrastructure projects is advantageous

About the Position The Senior ROSA counsellor holds a case load of more complex ROSA clients and provides the TIPS service. The Senior ROSA counsellor also holds the additional role of providing a high level of support to the Manager in meeting service agreement, organisational goals and provision of mentorship to other team members in program around practice development, case conceptualisation and service delivery. To work collaboratively with the Program Manager and ROSA team towards best practice and high-quality therapeutic intervention for children and young people subject to statutory involvement and their care team. The Counselling and Intervention (ROSA) services are provided to children and young people under 18 years who are subject to statutory intervention and require support as a therapeutic response arising from a personal experience of physical, emotional or sexual abuse/harm and neglect. Counselling support is a targeted therapeutic response involving skilled and qualified practitioners listening attentively and patiently to the express feeling and circumstances of the child (and carer), perceiving difficulties from a child’s point of view, helping the child to see things differently and facilitating choice and positive change in the child’s life, conducted within a private and safe setting. The overall objective of counselling and intervention services is to increase the stability of care placements and support to families where ongoing intervention by the Department is required. A discreet component of Counselling and Intervention services is Outreach Placement Support. This activity is aligned to Counselling and Intervention in that it aims to achieve placement stability, but the placement may be at home following a period of residential care. This is referred to as TIPS within Centacare NQ. Key Responsibilities Include Provide support to children and young people with more complex and sensitive needs. Provide the TIPS service for the ROSA program through an attachment theory lenses. Complete assessment of child or young person’s needs through engagement and consultation with the family, the school/day care as needed, child safety and the carer. Complete the My Star outcome and develop a therapy plan for intervention based on assessment. Ensure consistent communication to Child Safety, maintaining reporting requirements as outlined in Information Provision to the Department of children, Youth Justice and Multicultural Affairs. Regular review of intervention plan goals through completion of subsequent outcome stars and progress reports and meetings with Child Safety as per procedure. Maintain clients’ files in accordance with program and agency procedures. Ensure working from a trauma informed and child development practice framework and utilizing evidenced based interventions. Complete regular safety assessments and discuss any safety concerns with line manager immediately as per policy. Participate and contribute to line management meetings, supervision, team meetings (which occur twice a fortnight) and staff days as required. Attend ROSA waitlist acceptance meetings with manager and engage in appropriateness of the referrals as they are received from the Department. Complete required administration tasks and ensure waitlist and csnet data is put in correctly. Demonstrate leadership by coaching, mentoring and motivating team members to achieve program goals; including assisting with induction of new staff and supporting staff in case conceptualisation and planning and completing sit in as required. Support and assist line manager as required and where appropriate and take initiative when opportunities exist. This includes around program development and business planning, team building, supporting staff at meetings as directed by Manager. To ensure job flexibility the role may be required to perform any other duties as nominated by Centacare North Queensland consistent with the classification descriptors detailed in the Social, Community, Home Care and Disability Services Industry Award 2010.

Qualifications & Experience The Position Requires Possession of a relevant degree in social work of psychology and a minimum of three years’ experience working therapeutically with children and young people who have experienced trauma. Demonstrated ability to impart knowledge and skills using a range of theoretical frameworks in a creative and flexible way regarding trauma informed practice. Understanding of core therapeutic framework and approaches. which underpin the program which are attachment theory, Developmental theory, trauma focussed CBT , Narrative Therapy and Expressive Therapy. Highly developed communication skills both written and verbal for working with a broad-based network of service providers and culturally diverse clientele. Knowledge of and understanding of the issues that impact on Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people in contemporary society. Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively and sensitively with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. An ability to understand and describe the impact of childhood trauma on behaviour, emotional regulation, attachment and approaches to support the child or young person and care team understand these impacts and therapeutic approaches to support Demonstrated ability to undertake risk assessments relating to suicidal ideation and child protection concerns and capacity to liaise with manage to ensure client safety. Demonstrated organizational, time management and administration skills. Sound understanding of program procedures and the child protection system. Demonstrated ability of Leadership, guidance support and guidance to staff independently. Current open drivers’ licence. Current, and ability to retain, both a Working with Children Blue Card check and Disability services Yellow Card. Desirable Knowledge skills and experience in one or more of the following – Group work, community education and training and Indigenous services. Relevant experience in Community Services and or within a Not-For-Profit or Faith Based sectors.

The position will also provide appropriate groupwork interventions using a culturally congruent approach.

Relevant qualifications (Health Education Officer, Social Work, Psychology, Social Sciences, Youth Work or other relevant) or extensive experience working with Ezidi-speaking communities. 2. Thorough knowledge of the culture, history, and current issues of refugees from the Ezidi-speaking communities now residing in NSW. 3. Understanding of refugee issues and the effects of torture on individuals, families and communities with a particular focus on survivors of captivity. 4. Experience in community liaison, internal/external stakeholder liaison, community development and/or community work in Wagga Wagga. 5. Demonstrated Group Work skills and experience working with Yazidi/Ezidi women 6. Excellent oral, written & interpersonal communication skills and proficiency in the use of Microsoft Office and the ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team within a multicultural environment. 7. Bilingual in English and Ezidi and/or Kurmanji community language relevant to Wagga Wagga, NSW. 8. Current NSW Driver’s licence.

The role is responsible for managing wage subsidy eligibility, negotiation, documentation, and reimbursement processes to ensure all eligible placements receive timely and compliant support. By engaging directly with employers and internal teams, this role strengthens employer relationships, increases placement sustainability, and drives outcome conversion for TAG. The position ensures that all subsidy-related activities adhere to contractual, legislative, and quality standards while maintaining accurate records and supporting participants and employers throughout the lifecycle of a placement. Key Responsibilities: Identify and assess whether placements are eligible for subsidies in line with contractual and legislative requirements. Engage with employers to confirm eligibility, explain subsidy options, and support them through the documentation process. Negotiate subsidy structure and agreements with employers to ensure mutually beneficial outcomes. Provide guidance to employers and participants on required evidence and timeframes. Monitor subsidy milestones, including scheduled evidence requests and payment points. Request documentary evidence from employers at each payment due date. Conduct thorough quality checks on all documentation submitted to ensure accuracy and compliance. Identify and escalate discrepancies, risks, or non-compliant documentation. Prepare and submit subsidy payments to Finance for processing. Ensure all reimbursements are accurately recorded in WFAO and ESS or other required systems. Process wage subsidies and other eligible items in compliance with regulatory guidelines. Maintain strong understanding of Outcome and Documentary Evidence Guidelines. Maintain accurate and compliant records for all claims, subsidy documents, and employer communication. Ensure all Post Placement Support (PPS) and outcome-related evidence is recorded correctly. Protect confidentiality of participant and employer information. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure alignment and accuracy in claims processing. Build strong employer relationships to support placements and communication. Provide clear communication regarding evidence requirements and payment timelines. Participate in audits and compliance reviews. Identify opportunities to streamline processes. Take corrective actions to address compliance or performance issues

Competencies Experience as a Claims Officer or similar role. Background in disability employment or employment services. Experience with electronic document management systems. Knowledge of Outcome and Documentary Evidence Guidelines. About Training Alliance Group (TAG) Training Alliance Group (TAG) is a leading provider of employment and training services across Western Australia and a footprint in New South Wales. Through a range of government funded programs, including Workforce Australia, Inclusive Employment Australia, Transition to Work, Parent Pathways, Employability Skills Training and Self-Employment Assistance. TAG helps individuals overcome barriers and achieve long-term employment. Partnering with Registered Training Organisations (RTOs), ABV Training & Consulting (ABV) and Mining, Transport & Construction Services (MTACS), TAG also delivers nationally recognised vocational education in industries like business, construction, and community services. With a strong focus on inclusion, quality, and real outcomes, TAG is committed to building futures through meaningful support, training, and job opportunities. Why Join TAG? At Training Alliance Group, we value our people and invest in their growth, wellbeing, and success. When you join us, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits, including: Performance Incentives – Be rewarded for achieving great outcomes Employee Recognition Program – Celebrate milestones and success stories A well-structured fully supported induction program, with further development and training provided Refer-a-Friend Program – Earn rewards for helping us grow our team Employee Assistance Program (EAP) – Free, confidential wellbeing support Salary Sacrifice – Super – Maximise your superannuation benefits Professional Development Days – Dedicated time to focus on your growth Ongoing Learning & Development – Access training to build your skills and career Cash Out Annual Leave – Flexibility to suit your lifestyle and needs Annual Leave in Advance – Extra support when life requires it Make an Impact. Grow Your Career. Change Lives. Join a respected and expanding organisation where your work truly matters. At TAG, you’ll collaborate with supportive leaders, contribute to community-impacting initiatives, and thrive in a dynamic, purpose-driven environment. TAG is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, we welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, applicants from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, the LGBTIQ+ community and applicants who have a disability. If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know — we’re committed to a fair and inclusive experience for all. Join TAG and make a lasting difference—one career at a time. Apply Now!

Job Description: The Fundraising Officer is a vital team member responsible for supporting the implementation of our NFP's fundraising strategies to secure essential financial resources. This role primarily focuses on cultivating and stewarding relationships with a diverse range of donors, including individual supporters, community groups, and potentially smaller corporate or foundation prospects. You will assist with donor outreach, manage donor records, support fundraising campaigns and appeals, and contribute to ensuring a positive and impactful experience for all who support our cause.

Here are the job descriptions and skills/experience lists for a Community Engagement Officer and a Fundraising Officer, specifically tailored for an Australian Not-for-Profit (NFP) context: Community Engagement Officer Job Description: The Community Engagement Officer is a frontline role responsible for building, nurturing, and maintaining strong, authentic relationships with diverse community groups, volunteers, partners, and beneficiaries, all in support of our NFP's mission. This position actively listens to community needs, facilitates two-way communication, and helps implement engagement strategies through outreach, events, and programs. The goal is to foster active participation, gather valuable feedback, and ensure the community feels valued, heard, and deeply connected to the positive change we strive to achieve. Skills & Experience: Exceptional Interpersonal & Relationship Building: Proven ability to establish rapport, build trust, and maintain genuine connections with people from diverse backgrounds, including vulnerable populations and First Nations communities (if relevant to the NFP's work). Active Listening & Empathy: Strong capacity to understand and respond sensitively to community needs, concerns, and feedback. Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication for presenting information, facilitating discussions, and crafting accessible community messages. Event & Outreach Coordination: Experience in assisting with the planning, coordination, and delivery of community events, workshops, and outreach activities. Cultural Competence: Demonstrated understanding and respect for diverse cultural backgrounds, social contexts, and lived experiences within Australian communities. Problem-Solving & Adaptability: Ability to respond flexibly to community dynamics and resolve minor issues with a solutions-oriented approach. Networking & Collaboration: Capacity to identify opportunities for partnership and work collaboratively with internal teams and external community groups. Digital Engagement (Basic): Familiarity with using social media or other online platforms for community outreach and information sharing. Experience: 2-4 years of experience in a community-facing role, community development, social work, or a related field, ideally within an NFP or social services environment. Demonstrated experience in facilitating community participation or engagement activities. A strong understanding of, and commitment to, the NFP sector's values and ethical engagement principles. Relevant tertiary qualifications (e.g., Community Development, Social Sciences, Human Services) or equivalent practical experience. Desirable: Experience working with specific cohorts relevant to the NFP (e.g., youth, people with disability, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities). Current Australian Driver's License and willingness to travel within the service area. Current Working With Children Check (WWCC) and National Police Check (or willingness to obtain). Fundraising Officer Job Description: The Fundraising Officer is a vital team member responsible for supporting the implementation of our NFP's fundraising strategies to secure essential financial resources. This role primarily focuses on cultivating and stewarding relationships with a diverse range of donors, including individual supporters, community groups, and potentially smaller corporate or foundation prospects. You will assist with donor outreach, manage donor records, support fundraising campaigns and appeals, and contribute to ensuring a positive and impactful experience for all who support our cause. Skills & Experience: Donor-Centric Approach: A genuine passion for connecting with people and understanding their motivations for giving, with a focus on building lasting relationships. Communication & Persuasion: Excellent written and verbal communication skills for drafting compelling appeals, thank you letters, and donor reports, as well as engaging verbally with supporters. Organisational & Administrative Skills: Highly organised with strong attention to detail for managing donor data, campaign logistics, and administrative tasks. CRM Proficiency: Experience using donor management software (CRM) like Salesforce, Raiser's Edge, or similar systems for accurate record-keeping, tracking interactions, and generating reports. Project Support: Ability to assist with various fundraising activities, including direct mail appeals, digital campaigns, and event support. Initiative & Proactivity: A self-starter who can identify opportunities to engage donors and improve fundraising processes. Ethical Practice: A strong understanding and commitment to ethical fundraising practices and compliance with Australian charity regulations. Data Entry & Accuracy: Meticulous approach to data input and maintenance to ensure clean and reliable donor records. Experience: 1-3 years of experience in fundraising, donor relations, sales support, or a relevant administrative role, preferably within the NFP sector. Demonstrated experience in supporting fundraising campaigns or donor engagement activities. Familiarity with the fundraising cycle (identification, cultivation, solicitation, stewardship). Relevant tertiary qualifications (e.g., Marketing, Business, Communications, Non-profit Management) or equivalent practical experience. Understanding of the unique challenges and opportunities of fundraising in Australia.

1. Demonstrated knowledge of safe work practices 2. Previous cleaning experience 3. Ability to work independently and respond appropriately to situations including emergencies 4. Ability to follow care plans and perform duties in a timely manner 5. Good interpersonal skills, demonstrates empathy and confidentiality
Head of Strategy, Performance and Strategic Partnerships

Role purpose The Head of Strategy, Performance and Strategic Partnerships is responsible for coordinating the development and communication of the enterprise strategy, monitoring the enterprise strategy implementation; major project co-ordination and establishing strategic partnerships. Key responsibilities Strategy • Lead the development, refinement, and coordination of AICD’s integrated strategy, ensuring alignment with the organisation’s long-term vision. • Conduct market and competitor analysis to identify trends and opportunities • Define key performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor enterprise strategy implementation and organisation performance. • Lead strategic planning sessions with senior leaders to set clear, actionable goals. • Offer data-driven insights and recommendations to guide decision-making. Governance & Performance • Develop performance dashboards and reporting mechanisms to measure progress. • Identify areas for improvement and shape initiatives to close performance gaps. • Ensure departmental objectives and initiatives are aligned with broader organisational strategy. • Support senior leaders with regular performance reports and insights that inform executive and board discussions. • As required, lead or assist with projects forming part of the enterprise strategy Strategic Partnerships • Identify and establish strategic partnerships with key stakeholders in prioritised sectors. • Collaborate with strategic partners to develop mutually beneficial initiatives. • Build awareness, understanding and alignment across internal functions to enable AICD to maximise the value of strategic partnerships. • Develop internal capability to initiate, develop and maintain strategic partnerships. Team Management • Plan, control and execute budgets where applicable using knowledge, skills, and resources to meet budget. • Lead, develop, motivate and coach employees to enhance their performance, capability and development. • Support employees in performance development including completing annual performance appraisals to achieve their career goals. • Manage poor performance in accordance with AICD’s policies and procedures. Ensure records of actions taken of any performance action are well documented. • Oversee regular reviews of systems/processes to implement continuous improvements. AICD Contribution • Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. • Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. • Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis
Head of Strategy, Performance and Strategic Partnerships

Essential knowledge, skills and experience • Demonstrated enterprise-wide strategic thinking, and leadership, with the ability to identify opportunities and efficiencies across functions • Strong communication and influencing skills with senior leadership and external stakeholders, including conducting relationship based commercial negotiations • Proven experience in the development and implementation of long-term organisational strategy and goals. • Ability to act semi-autonomously as an enterprise leader • Expertise in business performance and insights that drive organisational performance improvements to inform strategic decision-making. • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills to address challenges and drive continuous improvements. • Extensive experience in identifying and assessing opportunities developing and managing strategic partnerships. • Strong commercial understanding of how strategy and partnerships drive organisational success. • Demonstrated experience in managing and developing teams, fostering a high-performance culture. Competencies • Adapting and Responding to Change • Writing and Reporting • Formulating Strategies and Concepts • Entrepreneurial and Commercial Thinking • Persuading and Influencing • Deciding and Initiating Action

The Head of Growth is responsible for driving the company’s strategic growth initiatives, including marketing, partnerships, and expansion efforts. They work closely with executive leadership to identify opportunities, optimize customer acquisition, and enhance the organization’s market presence.

Strategic thinking and experience in driving business growth through data-driven marketing and product initiatives Strong leadership across cross-functional teams including marketing, sales, and partnerships Proven track record of scaling customer acquisition, retention, and revenue Expertise in digital marketing, analytics, and performance optimisation Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to lead experimentation and innovation to unlock growth opportunities Experience in developing strategic partnerships and expanding into new markets

The Volunteer Coordinator at Company Name plays a crucial role in the management and support of volunteers, ensuring that all activities run smoothly and efficiently. This position involves a variety of responsibilities aimed at enhancing the volunteer experience and maximising their contributions to the organisation. Key Responsibilities: Recruit, train, and supervise volunteers, providing them with necessary orientation and resources. Develop and implement volunteer programmes that align with the organisation's goals and objectives. Maintain clear communication with volunteers, addressing queries and concerns in a timely manner. Organise volunteer recognition events to celebrate contributions and foster engagement within the team. Monitor volunteer activities to ensure compliance with policies, procedures, and safety regulations. Create schedules for volunteer shifts, ensuring adequate coverage for events and projects. Collaborate with other departments to identify opportunities for volunteer involvement in various initiatives. Prepare reports on volunteer engagement metrics to assess programme effectiveness and areas for improvement. This role is essential for fostering a positive relationship between the organisation and its volunteers, ensuring that they feel valued while contributing to worthwhile causes within the community. The ideal candidate will possess strong organisational skills, excellent interpersonal abilities, and a passion for community service.

Required skills Strong organisational and time management skills Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities Ability to motivate and engage volunteers Proficiency in using volunteer management software and databases Conflict resolution and problem-solving skills Attention to detail and accuracy in record-keeping Required experiences Previous experience in volunteer coordination or a related field Experience in community engagement or non-profit organisations Demonstrated ability to develop and implement training programmes for volunteers Experience with event planning and execution involving volunteers A proven track record of managing multiple projects simultaneously Prior experience in fundraising or donor relations is advantageous

The Hospitality Manager is responsible for overseeing the operations of a hospitality establishment such as a hotel or restaurant. This includes managing staff members, ensuring customer satisfaction, and maintaining the financial health of the business. The Hospitality Manager will also be responsible for developing and implementing marketing strategies to attract new customers and retain existing customers.

Bachelors degree in Hospitality Management or related field. Extensive experience in hospitality operations and management. Strong leadership and team management skills. Excellent communication and customer service skills. Ability to develop and implement operational strategies and plans. Knowledge of industry regulations, policies, and procedures. Experience in budgeting, financial management, and cost control. Strong organizational and time-management skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and handle multiple tasks. Experience in managing inventory, purchasing, and supply chain operations. Familiarity with hospitality technology and software. Flexibility to work evenings, weekends, and holidays.

Primarily ensures the quality of locally manufactured products meets technical guidelines, drawings, and specifications. Secondarily ensures that incoming raw materials and finished goods adhere to company standards before integration. The nature and scope of the role is to work under limited supervision within routines, methods, and procedures where some discretion and judgment are required. Job Responsibilities: 1. Primary Focus: Production Quality Control & Compliance Data Metrics & Analytics: Captures data on failures and process performance, translating it into usable graphical information and test information reports to drive continuous improvement. Issue Logging & Facilitation: Logs quality issues and actively facilitates their progression, ensuring robust containment, proper disposition, thorough root-cause analysis, and final corrective actions. Production Sampling: Executes systematic production sampling inspections across manufacturing runs to verify ongoing quality consistency. ESD Compliance: Conducts regular Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) compliance verifications across production lines to safeguard sensitive components. Documentation Verification: Performs rigorous production documentation compliance verifications to ensure line logs, traveller sheets, and build records are accurately completed. Line Inspections: Performs Surface Mount Device (SMD) line inspections for reel placement and component selection for printed circuit board (PCB) production runs. Manufacturing Monitoring: Monitors ongoing locally and externally manufactured product quality based on defined technical guidelines and checklists developed in conjunction with Engineering. Cross-functional Liaison: Liaises actively with Engineering, Warehouse, and Production staff to facilitate production quality outcomes and corrective actions in a suitable timeframe. 2. Primary Focus: Defect Containment, Sorting & NCR Support Issue Logging & Facilitation: Logs production quality issues and actively facilitates their progression through the material management system. Containment & Disposition: Coordinates immediate quarantine and containment of non-conforming materials on the manufacturing floor to prevent further production escape. Sorting & Rework Support: Executes and oversees targeted sorting activities of in-process components and assemblies when defects are identified, collaborating on rework actions. Root-Cause Analysis: Participates in basic root-cause analysis and defect investigation alongside engineering teams. CAPA Progression: Facilitates the implementation and verification of immediate corrective actions on the line to ensure sustained resolution of process discrepancies. Priority Identification: Reviews the Incoming Material List and identifies priorities for incoming material inspection based on production needs. Order Review: Reviews incoming material details, such as purchase order numbers and quantities, to verify delivery accuracy. Technical Reference Gathering: Obtains relevant reference samples and engineering drawings for the purpose of inspecting raw materials and finished goods destined for Manufacturing and Wholesale. Dimensional & Finish Checks: Inspects incoming parts and performs critical dimension and finishes checks using precision instrumentation and measurement tools, referencing drawings, specifications, and AQL guidelines. Defect Reporting: Reports on problems identified with incoming material by generating Rejected Material Advice forms within the company’s material management system. 3. Secondary Focus: Inwards Goods & Incoming Inspection 4. General & Regulatory Compliance QA Support: Undertakes tasks related to quality matters and issues as required by the department manager in the evolution of the company. General: Executes any other duties as directed to support business objectives.

Experience: Technically skilled with experience in manufacturing and warehouse organisations, with a strong background working within established Quality Management Systems (QMS). Analytical Skills: Basic proficiency in data handling, defect logging, and presenting manufacturing metrics graphically. Attributes: Broadly "hands-on" with a proven ability to work independently under limited supervision as well as collaboratively within a cross-functional team. Communication: Computer literate with excellent written and verbal communication skills. Organisation: Well-organised with strong time management and prioritisation skills.

T4.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Title",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Title",''))) AS "Job Role Title",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Description",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Description",''))) AS "Job Role Description",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Identifier",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Identifier",''))) AS "Job Role Identifier",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Job Role Skills and Experience",''), NULLIF(u."Job Role Skills and Experience",''))) AS "Job Role Skills and Experience",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Created Date", u."Job Role Created Date")) AS "Job Role Created Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Job Role Modified Date", u."Job Role Modified Date")) AS "Job Role Modified Date"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.GENERAL_JOB_ROLE u USING ("Job Role ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
